summary: Native NuGet packages for Win32 increased from ~13 MB to ~75 MB and Linux from ~39 MB to ~54 MB in 3.119.2 due to native PDB files being included in packages after PR #3374 expanded the IsNativeAssetsProject symbol packaging to all platforms.

stepsToReproduce: Compare size of native assets in runtimes/ folder to 3.119.1
Observe ~5.7x increase on Windows and ~1.4x increase on Linux

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The native PDB files are still included in packages. The IsNativeAssetsProject property and IncludeSymbols=true remain in binding/NativeAssets.Build.targets.

regression.reason: PR #3374 (commit 6a7a452af) expanded the IsNativeAssetsProject symbol packaging from Windows-only to all platforms, and removed ExcludeSymbolsFromPackage=false while not setting SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg. This causes native PDB files to be bundled in the main NuGet package.

analysis.summary: Native PDB files (~100 MB per Windows architecture) are included in the main NuGet packages due to PR #3374 expanding the IsNativeAssetsProject packaging mechanism to all platforms. The Win32 native output directory contains libSkiaSharp.pdb (80 MB) and libHarfBuzzSharp.pdb (20 MB) per architecture, and the NativeAssetPackageFile glob (libSkiaSharp*) matches both DLL and PDB files. Without SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg, the IncludeSymbols=true setting causes PDBs to end up in the main .nupkg rather than a separate symbols package.
analysis.rationale: This is a packaging regression, not a functional bug. The native libraries themselves are unchanged, but the NuGet packages are 5-6x larger on Windows due to including native debug symbols. The root cause is clearly traced to PR #3374 which generalized the NativeAssets packaging. macOS is unaffected because its output directory has no PDB files and doesn't use IsNativeAssetsProject.

keySignals.text: Win32: 13,051 KB → 75,014 KB, Linux: 39,466 KB → 53,908 KB, macOS: 6,934 KB (unchanged)
keySignals.interpretation: Massive Win32 increase aligns with PDB file sizes (~100 MB per arch). macOS unaffected confirms the issue is platform-specific to the packaging mechanism.

keySignals.text: Whatever change happened seemed to land before preview 1
keySignals.interpretation: Consistent with PR #3374 being merged before any 3.119.2 preview.

keySignals.text: It seems that the new version includes pdb files in package.
keySignals.interpretation: Community member correctly identified the root cause and provided an MSBuild workaround.

codeInvestigation.finding: IsNativeAssetsProject condition sets IncludeSymbols=true and BuildOutputTargetFolder=runtimes. PR #3374 expanded this from IsWindowsNativeAssets to IsNativeAssetsProject, applying it to all native asset projects.
PDB/DBG files are separated into _TargetPathsToSymbols. However, without SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg, the default NuGet SDK behavior includes these in the main package.
NativeAssetPackageFile glob 'libSkiaSharp*' matches both .dll and .pdb files. The Windows output directory contains libSkiaSharp.pdb (80 MB) and libHarfBuzzSharp.pdb (20 MB) per architecture.
Build script explicitly copies libSkiaSharp.pdb to the output directory alongside libSkiaSharp.dll. This has been present for a long time but previously only affected symbol packages.
AllowedOutputExtensionsInPackageBuildOutputFolder includes .pdb, which allows PDB files in the managed build output folder of NuGet packages.

workarounds: Add MSBuild targets to delete PDB files from runtimes/ after build and publish (workaround from Jack251970 in issue comments)
nextQuestions: Should native PDB files be in a separate symbols package (snupkg) or excluded entirely?
Is the Linux size increase from versioned .so files (libSkiaSharp.so.119.0.0) being included alongside libSkiaSharp.so, or from expanded architecture support?
Should SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg be set globally for NativeAssets projects?

resolution.hypothesis: PR #3374 generalized the NativeAssets packaging and IncludeSymbols=true without setting SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg, causing native PDB files to be bundled in the main NuGet package instead of a separate symbols package.

proposals.title: Set SymbolPackageFormat to snupkg
proposals.description: Add SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg to the IsNativeAssetsProject PropertyGroup in NativeAssets.Build.targets. This moves PDB files to a separate .snupkg that is only downloaded by symbol servers, not by normal NuGet restore.

proposals.title: Exclude PDB files from NativeAssetPackageFile globs
proposals.description: Change the Win32 csproj globs from 'libSkiaSharp*' to 'libSkiaSharp*.dll' (and similar for HarfBuzzSharp). This prevents PDB files from being picked up at all.

proposals.title: MSBuild target to remove PDB files from output
proposals.description: Add AfterTargets Build/Publish targets to delete runtimes/**/*.pdb files from the output directory.

resolution.recommendedReason: This is the standard NuGet approach for shipping debug symbols separately. It preserves debugging capability via symbol servers while keeping the main package small.

actionability.reason: Root cause is identified (PR #3374 IncludeSymbols expansion), but the correct fix approach needs verification — whether to use snupkg, exclude PDBs entirely, or narrow the glob patterns.

actions.description: Apply bug, build, and platform labels
Acknowledge the report, confirm root cause, mention workaround
actions.comment:
Thanks for flagging this — the size increase is confirmed.

The root cause is PR #3374, which expanded native symbol packaging to all platforms. On Windows, this pulls in `libSkiaSharp.pdb` (~80 MB) and `libHarfBuzzSharp.pdb` (~20 MB) per architecture into the main NuGet package. Previously these were only in separate symbol packages.

In the meantime, @Jack251970's workaround above (MSBuild targets to delete PDB files from the output) is a solid stopgap. We'll look at setting `SymbolPackageFormat=snupkg` so that debug symbols go to a separate symbols package rather than the main `.nupkg`.

actions.description: Link to PR #3374 which introduced the regression
